title: plumeria xanthostoma
intro: Plumeria xanthostoma, also known as Yellow Frangipani, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, where it is highly valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, thanks to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties, which promote relaxation and relieve congestion. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Plumeria xanthostoma can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, offering a range of health benefits and natural remedies.
